messages.content:
What was observed
- R2 Serial0/1/0 was administratively down and R2 showed no OSPF neighbor toward R1; R1 reported the link down / no neighbor.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Root cause: Serial0/1/0 on R2 was administratively shut (so R2 could not receive OSPF hellos). A historical inbound ACL that blocked OSPF multicast was also present and may have contributed earlier.

Changes made
- Removed the inbound ACL from R2 Serial0/1/0.
- Brought R2 Serial0/1/0 up (set interface state to up).

Verification: passed/failed and what remains
- Interface state verification: PASSED — R2 Serial0/1/0 now admin up, oper up.
- OSPF adjacency verification: NOT YET PASSED — R1 still reports the Serial0/1/0 link as Down in the latest OSPF snapshot. Remaining: confirm OSPF neighbor state on both routers (show ip ospf neighbor / get_ospf) to ensure R1 and R2 reach FULL. If adjacency does not form, provide post‑change OSPF neighbor outputs and any remaining ACL or interface counters for further troubleshooting.
